quote_text: The "stop-the-clock" Directive postpones the entry into application of the reporting requirements for those companies that were previously required to report for the first time for financial years 2025 or 2026.
A separate "stop the clock" proposal will also postpone by two years the reporting requirements for companies currently in the scope of CSRD which were scheduled to report as of 2026 or 2027.

The standard adopted in the recommendation will reduce administrative burden on SMEs by making it easier for them to respond to requests for sustainability information from large companies and financial institutions.
ensure that sustainability reporting requirements for large companies will not burden smaller companies in their value chains

This State aid, which corresponds to 15% (taxable) of the amount invested each year, may be claimed by private-sector companies that are legally established and conduct their main business in the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg.
